description: My experience with TeamViewer has been positive. It is easy to setup, reliable, and secure, making remote access simple and stress free. The connection quality is excellent and features are well thought out.
author: Junaid E.
pros: I liked how easy it is to use TeamViewer even with people who are not tech-savy. TeamViewer offers great value for money, helping save time and reduce travel cost. I found easy and quick to share files with TeamViewer compare to the platform I used in the past.
cons: TeamViewer is fast and reliable, but mobile phone support is pricey for occasional users. A pay as you go option would make it much more appealing.

review_title: The Most Advanced IT Service Desk Remote Control Tool!
description: TeamViewer has made such a positive impact on my ability to troubleshoot and resolve IT Service Desk tickets remotely with all of it's advanced features, it is easily the most comprehensive and impressive system I have used in end user support. TeamViewer is the only remote control system I know that allows you to switch screens seamlessly, sent the control + alt + delete function if you are trying to unlock a device remotely, and allow you to disable remote input from the end user if you need to do a hard fix on something. It is a very innovative system I wholeheartedly recommend for businesses.
author: Cameron T.
pros: TeamViewer is the most seamless and well developed IT remote control tool I have used in my time of being an IT Service Desk Analyst, packed with lots of advanced features that allow you to communicate with the end user you are helping, as well as advanced features that allow you to make your experience as an administrator a lot easier such as switching screens or disabling remote input if needed.
cons: TeamViewer regularly updates it's user interface, meaning that some useful features can be hard to find post update. This is not a major issue because you can see why each user interface update is being done, and that is to push a more refined and complete user experience, so once you are used to it you start to embrace these changes.

review_title: Good product destroyed by removal of core functionality and reliability
description: It used to be good and useful, if a bit expensive tool, but then they decided to make a new, worse version, so now it is a frustration to use.
author: Vesa R.
pros: The old interface and funtionality was good and clear, with very few quirks and connections worked most of the time fine. It used to be a master of remote control
cons: The new interface is a mess, with missing funtionality in basics, including things like sorting all devices by group. It is trying to be all tools crammed into one UI and thus bad at all of them. They should really make a remote control only UI with all the funtionality of the old one. Also the new one seems to fail connections a LOT.

description: Horrible termination experience with huge penalties for not advising them before deciding not to continue subscription
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Expensive, the T&Cs are a must read as they have they are not standard and could cause a surprise cost on termination A great pro if you like unexpected bills.
cons: When you stop paying, rather than expire your usage like all other subscriptions. Teamviewer T&C states you need to advise them in writing. If you don't they take it to a debtcollector with huge collection fees. Buyer beware - I strongly recommend that anyone considering their services pay close attention to the fine print and be prepared for an unpleasant billing process if you ever decide to leave.

review_title: A Practical Look at TeamViewer’s Strengths and Shortcomings.
description: My overall experience with TeamViewer has been consistently strong, mainly because it delivers dependable, secure remote access with very little friction, whether connecting across operating systems or helping someone troubleshoot from afar. Its performance is smooth in most situations, and features like file transfer, multi‑monitor support, and session recording make it feel like a complete remote‑work toolkit rather than just a screen‑sharing app. At the same time, the platform isn’t without frustrations: pricing can feel high, the interface occasionally feels cluttered, and false commercial‑use flags can interrupt work at inconvenient moments. Even so, once everything is running, TeamViewer proves to be a reliable, versatile solution that makes remote support and collaboration straightforward.
author: Lucas B.
pros: TeamViewer’s biggest strength is how effortless and reliable remote access feels, especially when connecting across different devices and operating systems without any complicated setup. Its smooth performance, even on slower networks, makes it easy to troubleshoot issues or collaborate in real time, and features like file transfer, multi‑monitor support, and session recording add a lot of practical value. I also appreciated the strong focus on security, with encryption and access controls that make remote sessions feel safe and trustworthy. The overall experience is streamlined, intuitive, and dependable, which makes it a standout tool for both personal and professional use.
cons: TeamViewer’s main drawbacks come from its pricing model, which can feel steep for individuals or small teams and sometimes triggers false commercial‑use warnings that interrupt sessions. The interface, while generally clean, can feel cluttered with secondary features, making simple tasks take longer than expected. Performance can also vary when connecting to older devices or unstable networks, and occasional connection restrictions—like cooldown periods after long sessions—can be frustrating when you’re trying to work quickly. What part of your overall experience would you like to describe next?

review_title: Not worth it...look elsewhere for cheaper and easier products.
description: Teamviewer product...good...Teamviewer corporate structure with their latest money grab...I give them 0 stars for that.
author: Curt H.
pros: The remote support program looked good while we used it...for many years...but the corporate structure is another thing altogether.
cons: Corporate structure. Appears that there has been a change in environment and they are getting desperate...so now they are trying to get more money from everyone. When I told them that this was no consistent with their agreements...they started quoting something. I asked for a copy of what they were supposedly quoting...AND THEY COULD NOT DO IT. I was told that my license was not going to be valid any longer...yet it was a perpetual license which was supposed to be good as long as the product was supported by the OS. They said I could...but internally only...I told them that this would not work for me and if they wanted to change and make it not available any longer that they could just give me a new license. They refused. The cost of the product is absolutely outrageous in comparison to other products. The only reason I was staying with them was because it was good at what it did...BUT...their new changes required me to pay $1000's of dollars more to continue to use. Try working with their support...they simply stand behind their statements even though they can provide no documentation, etc to support their position....yet I could provide documentation showing where I was coming from....hhhhmmmm...go figure. TERRIBLE COMPANY...go elsewhere.

description: allows me to access my work devices in the office from home and remotely securely and instantly , this is an invaluable tool when working from home or when an urgent matter arises
author: joanne F.
pros: I loke the instant access to my devices securely , I also like the fact that team viewer works across different platforms and is user friendly
cons: some times the connection freezes or slows down , this can be frustration whilst working to a tight busy schedule

description: Overall TeamViewer is a good product for file sharing and its very easy to use especially indicated for a large group of workers
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: What I loke the most about TeamViewer is that its a great file sharing, works well in a large team with team collaboration and task management.
cons: What I like the least about TeamViewer is that sometimes it might present some bugs or errors in the system
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: It is ok, I am not an operator of it, I use it from the other side of fence. However, it gets the job done
author: Kayla W.
pros: I like how someone external can log into my computer and take control. Which makes it good as they do not have to be physically next to me
cons: If the operator or myself is typing you cannot see if someone is typing so something the words overlap

description: I like it because it is easy to share files, it makes teamwork easier by having access to other computers
author: ALFREDO S.
pros: Easy to use, a quick way to be able to connect to other computers. It doesn't matter where you are. customer support is great.
cons: has everything, I couldn't say anything negative. bugs and issues solved in day-to-day excellent system.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall, the product has always worked very well and have not had issues with it. Very reliable and can always count on it.
author: Kris D.
pros: Very easy to quickly resolve issues with employees by jumping on their computer whenever they have an issue.
cons: Not a fan of the new interface. It would be nice if it defaulted to online devices only, so I wouldn't need to do it manually every time.

review_title: Reliable and Secure Remote Access Solution :-)
description: My overall experience with TeamViewer has been excellent. It provides secure, fast, and reliable remote access, making it easy to support users and troubleshoot issues from anywhere. The interface is intuitive, and the cross-platform compatibility ensures flexibility in various work scenarios. It has significantly improved our efficiency and response time.
author: Rafique S.
pros: TeamViewer is extremely reliable and user-friendly, making remote access and support effortless. I especially appreciate its fast connection speed, secure environment, and cross-platform compatibility, which allow us to support users anytime, anywhere without disruptions. Its intuitive interface and stable performance greatly improve our productivity.
cons: Occasionally, the connection can be slow depending on network conditions, and the licensing costs can be on the higher side for small teams. However, overall performance and reliability remain excellent.

description: TeamViewer is the best choice for normal computers but is has problems on IoT devices like Raspberry Pi 5.
author: Robert A.
pros: Good-quality product, helpful customer service, easy to manage device and customer data, stability, user-friendly
cons: * TeamViewer software uses slots / operating system, not MAC address or device: I have a dualboot (Windows-Linux) setup and if I install the software on both operating systems, it takes 2 slots instead of 1 although both OS runs on the same physical device; * Doesn't work on Raspberry OS (on Raspberry Pi 5 device): it is installed successfully but I can't connect to my Pi device. I tried to connect using the Raspberry Pi's IP address from a Windows 10 Pro-based notebook. It works correctly on X86/64-based architecture but not on Arm processor.

description: It is good except for my complaint listed here above. Maybe you could provide more information on how to get a faster start-up. The fibre connectivity at the office is 50Mbps and at my home also 50Mbps up and down.
author: Gunther B.
pros: I use TeamViewer for business purposes to access remote computers, such as my desktop and the server at the office.
cons: I have a problem with the latest version of TeamViewer, because it takes too long to connect with my desktop or server. The layout is different and much slower to the previous version, because the new version doesn't show immediately that TeamViewer has registered my attempt to connect to the remote site, often I click twice or three times before something happens. In some instances I end up having three connections of which two have to be cancelled.

description: I love TeamViewer and recommend it constantly to those who experience issues of connectivity while working remotely; many coworkers complain that other services are unreliable, so I always tell them about TeamViewer.
author: Marj O.
pros: TeamViewer is the best service to keep all my devices connected while I process tasks for both my job and for my volunteer work. It's a lifesaver when I'm forced to work from home as well.
cons: When I have contacted TeamViewer in the past for service assistance, the instructions have been convoluted with unnecessary steps involved or steps provided out of order with excess information that was not pertinent to the task as hand. They need to work on making that process more concise, and just follow a simple chronological order without embellishments to get customers up and running as quickly as possible.

description: Overall, very satisfied. I have my data capturer using Zoho due to the fact that I have only one license for multiple connections. It works for her, so we are all happy.
author: Madeleen R.
pros: The resolution on pc you are connecting to is much better than on ie Anydesk. File transfer is also very easy via Teamviewer. Much better value for money than any other for what you get. I use Teamviewer only.
cons: I do not like the new interface at all. Still using the old one. The resolution has to be adjusted on connecting pc to work and that annoys the client.

description: Overall it has been a fantastic tool that we use to support our clients. TeamViewer has regular updates and any vulnerabilities are patched very quickly. The software is quite expensive but a reliable connection is worth it.
author: Wasek T.
pros: The software has a brilliant user interface. Very easy to remote into a users machine and reliable connection. Multiple features such has file transfer, chat box, switch between multiple screens.
cons: Sometimes there has been lags during a remote session and have noticed that it can be a bit resource intensive which can be an issue on some entry level machines. Navigating through settings can be difficult at first as it is a bit cluttered.

description: Overall it was a very positive experience. I honestly didn't have a lot of issues, and any issues I did have, I was able to contact Support and they were able to assist.
author: Tracy A.
pros: It was easy to use and log into. Always had timely responses from Support if ever I did have an issue.
cons: Would sometimes lose connectivity or wasn't able to log in, but not sure that was a TeamViewer issue or if it was a server issue.

description: I started using a free version during the pandemic. I liked the features so much, that I purchased it and am still using it to this day.
author: Shelley M.
pros: Ease of connection. I am not a computer genius, and I have no problem connecting. I had an issue awhile back and it was easy to contact TeamViewer and have my problem solved.
cons: It is a little expensive, since I work for a nonprofit. But I do believe the value of it is worth the money.

review_title: Work Made Easier from Different Places
description: Overall I am satisfied. It's complicated and not always user friendly but your staff help bridge that gap.
author: Charles F.
pros: Having tech support when needed. Ease of getting remote access when needed and excellent file transfer ability. That is my favorite feature. I was literally able to use a scoring app on one computer while testing someone from the other. Very versitile and makes me able to work from 2 locations at once.
cons: At times it refuses Easy Access and demands the other machine's pw which is always changing. Then easy access later resumes and I'm able to use it.

description: TeamViewer has always been great to work with. Overall I give them 2 thumbs up. So far they have fixed every problem I've had.
author: JoLynn C.
pros: TeamViewer always helps me no matter the problem. They are fast and very knowledgeable. I love that they can get in to my computer and fix it.
cons: I don't like that I have to call sometimes. There are times my team viewer won't hook up or stay hooked up.

review_title: Still the king of Remote Support!
description: My overall experience with Teamviewer is very good. I have used many other applications that try to do what you do but they are not as easy to use.
author: Mark S.
pros: Easy to gain access remotely without having to instruct the user to install the full software. Once you are connected everything is easy to navigate especially multiple screens. Transferring files to the remote computer is effortless.
cons: Being locked out of a system if you are prompted for elevated admin credentials . Gaining this control is cumbersome as the option for Windows Authentication is not visible.

description: he platform is reliable, easy to use, and has made remote access and support smooth and efficient. I appreciate how quickly I can connect to any device and resolve issues without disruption. Overall, TeamViewer has been a great tool, and I’m very satisfied with how well it supports our daily workflow and technical needs.
author: katie S.
pros: It’s easy to connect remotely without any complicated steps. The interface is simple and user-friendly. Connections are fast and reliable.
cons: The support is sometimes a long time to have an issue resolved. I have had to get a new computer a few times this year and re-setting up team viewer has been a pain.

description: Customer service is excellent! Product is easy to use and efficient. I would highly recommend it to anyone.
author: Melodie A.
pros: Easy to use and has a long time-out. Customer Service is USA based and very helpful. I would highly recommend it.
cons: nothing really other than it's a bit expense. Sometime I go to use it and it has logged me out which is inconvenient but easily fixed

description: I've had a positive overall experience using TeamViewer through the past 15+ years. It allows me to keep working remotely.
author: Linda P.
pros: Using TeamViewer is very easy to log onto, very few issues & great support teams when there is an issue.
cons: Sometimes I'll get kicked off & I never know what the reason is, but it's easy to log back into the program.
